modelInstruction: Write all user-visible output in English.
notEnoughActivityFull: Not enough activity for this period, or Apple Intelligence is unavailable.
noMemoryFoundFull: No memory found for this period.
notSeenOnScreen: I did not see that on screen in the retrieved excerpts.
dashboardConnectionText: The macOS window is open, but the embedded local server is not responding yet on %@.
dashboardConnectionHelp: Quit and reopen ScreenMemory from the menu icon. If the problem persists, port 8790 may already be in use.
briefPermissionHeadline: Allow Screen Recording to turn this dashboard into a live memory.
briefPausedHeadline: Memory is paused. Existing memories remain searchable, but nothing new is indexed.
briefStoppedHeadline: Capture is stopped. You can still search history, but the stream is not current.
briefResumeThreadsOne: 1 thread to resume from recent traces.
briefResumeThreadsOther: %d threads to resume from recent traces.
briefIndexedToday: %@ indexed today. The timeline is ready to query.
briefNoActivityHeadline: No usable activity for this day. Start capture and come back after a few minutes.
resumeFromJournal: From the latest generated work journal.
signalCaptureUnauthorizedDetail: macOS blocks Screen Recording. Nothing new will be indexed.
signalIndexingPausedDetail: Pause protects your screen, but ScreenMemory can no longer help proactively.
signalCaptureStoppedDetail: The dashboard can search history, but active memory is off.
signalNoChunksDetail: Start capture or run reindexing to feed search.
signalGenerationUnavailableDetail: Search and metrics still work. RAG summaries are waiting for Apple Intelligence.
signalFragmentedDayDetail: %@/h context switches. The brief should help recover the thread.
loginUnavailable: login item unavailable (%@). Launch from the packaged app.
loginRequiresApproval: requires approval (System Settings -> General -> Login Items)
menuGrantThenRelaunch: grant permission, then relaunch the app
permText: Capture cannot run until you grant permission. Nothing is indexed.
timeline: Usable timeline filter by app, window, or topic
askHint: Enter = RAG answer (about 10-30 s, local model) · Search = instant raw matches
warn: The model did not find the answer in retrieved memories. It refuses to invent. Rephrase or narrow the period.
coachOpus: Coach Opus claude -p --model opus · web research · 30-day anti-repeat
coachFilter: filter by project, app, client, topic...
opusIntro: Opus will receive relevant metrics, sessions, OCR excerpts, and monthly advice history to avoid repeats. It can run targeted web research on detected technologies and tools.
footer: encrypted local database · Neural Engine embeddings · on-device generation by default · Opus only on explicit click
timelineStill: The timeline remains available to resume from a precise context.
tryFilter: Try another filter or check that capture is running.
captureNeeded: Capture must run to accumulate data.
journalNeeded: Start capture to generate your journal.
regeneratingSessions: map-reducing sessions with the local model...
instantPreview: Instant session preview. Click Regenerate to ask the local model for the summary.
modelUnavailable: Apple Intelligence is unavailable or too slow to produce this text.
coachAnalyzing: analyzing the day with the local model...
metricsOnly: Metrics are computed instantly. Click Regenerate to ask the local model for advice.
notEnoughActivity: not enough activity for that day
opusWorking: claude -p --model opus with filtered context, monthly history, and web research...
weekRegenerating: rolling up the last 7 days with the local model...
weeklyMetrics: Weekly metrics are computed instantly. Click Regenerate to ask the local model for the synthesis.
noTrend: not enough activity in this period
trendNeed: Capture must run for several days to draw a trend.
activeDeepFocus: Active time, deep work, and focus score
permissionAlert: If permission was just granted, quit and reopen ScreenMemory from the menu to start capture.
timeout: timeout: the local model or database did not respond fast enough
